name: expresskit
description: Server-side only. Lightweight Express.js wrapper integrating with NodeKit for structured request logging, distributed tracing, async middleware, CSRF, CSP headers, and declarative route definitions.
when_to_use: Server-side only
avoid: Not for browser/frontend code — server-side Node.js only. Not for non-NodeKit Express apps — requires a NodeKit instance.
not_for: Not for browser/frontend code — server-side Node.js only. Not for non-NodeKit Express apps — requires a NodeKit instance.

name: ExpressKit Overview
description: ExpressKit is a lightweight Express.js wrapper that integrates with NodeKit to provide structured request logging, distributed tracing, async middleware/controller support, CSRF protection, CSP headers, language detection, and a declarative route configuration DSL.
when_to_use: When building a NodeKit-integrated Express HTTP server with structured logging and tracing
For services that need declarative route definitions with built-in auth policy, CSP, and CSRF support
avoid: For non-NodeKit Express apps — ExpressKit requires a NodeKit instance
When a minimal Express setup is needed without the full NodeKit stack
content:
Usage pattern:
```typescript
const nodekit = new NodeKit();
const app = new ExpressKit(nodekit, routes, setup?);
app.run(); // starts listening
```
Routes are a Record<string, AppRouteDescription | AppRouteHandler>. Route keys are 'METHOD /path' (e.g. 'GET /api/items'). Setup callback gets express, setupBaseMiddleware, setupParsers, setupRoutes, setupLangMiddleware, setupErrorHandlers for custom ordering.

Each request gets req.ctx (child AppContext) and req.originalContext for the full request span.
Each route can specify: handler, authPolicy, handlerName, beforeAuth, afterAuth, cspPresets, enableCaching, disableSelfStats.

name: ExpressKit
description: The main ExpressKit class that creates a configured Express application. Automatically sets up base middleware (request ID, logging, compression), language detection, body parsers, routes with auth/CSRF/CSP, and error handlers. Exposes run() to start the HTTP server.
when_to_use: As the HTTP server layer in a NodeKit application
signature: new ExpressKit(nodekit: NodeKit, routes: AppRoutes, setup?: (params: SetupParams) => void): ExpressKit

name: csp
description: A utility object for building Content Security Policy presets. Provides preset factory functions (none, self, inline, eval, nonce, data, dynamic) and references to CSP directive constants and values.
when_to_use: When configuring expressCspPresets in AppConfig to define Content Security Policy for routes or the whole app
avoid: Calling these at request time — define presets statically in config.ts
signature: csp: { directives, values, none(), self(), inline(), eval(), nonce(), data(), dynamic(), makeCspObject() }

name: AuthPolicy
description: An enum that defines the authentication policy for ExpressKit routes. Values: `disabled` (no auth check), `optional` (auth checked but not required), `required` (unauthenticated requests redirected or rejected).
when_to_use: Setting authPolicy on individual AppRouteDescription or globally via AppConfig.appAuthPolicy
Defining which routes require authentication and which are public
avoid: Do not use AuthPolicy values as strings — import the enum to avoid typos

name: getContract
description: Retrieves the RouteContract associated with a given AppRouteHandler. Returns undefined if the handler was not wrapped with withContract. Useful for building OpenAPI/Swagger documentation or validation middleware that inspects route contracts at registration time.
when_to_use: Generating API documentation by iterating over registered routes and their contracts
In middleware or tooling that needs to inspect a route's request/response schema
avoid: At request time — contracts are registered once at startup

name: getErrorContract
description: Retrieves the ErrorContract associated with a given AppErrorHandler. Returns undefined if the handler was not wrapped with withErrorContract. Mirrors getContract for error handlers.
when_to_use: Generating API error documentation by inspecting registered error handler contracts
avoid: At request time — error contracts are registered at startup

name: setLang
description: Sets the user language on an AppContext if the language is allowed by AppConfig.appAllowedLangs. Returns true if the language was set, false if it was rejected. Used in middleware or auth handlers to persist the detected language to the request context.
when_to_use: In language detection middleware to store the resolved language on req.ctx
In auth handlers when language is determined from user profile or Accept-Language header
avoid: Do not call outside a request context — setLang requires an AppContext from req.ctx
signature: function setLang({ lang, ctx }: { lang: string; ctx: AppContext }): boolean

name: ValidationError
description: An AppError subclass thrown when Zod request validation fails in withContract. Carries the ZodError instance for detailed field-level error reporting. Caught by the global error handler to return a structured 400 response.
when_to_use: Throwing explicitly when manual validation fails inside a withContract handler (manualValidation: true)
Catching in the AppConfig.appValidationErrorHandler to customize the 400 response format
avoid: For business logic errors — use AppError from @gravity-ui/nodekit directly
signature: class ValidationError extends AppError { zodError?: ZodError; constructor(message: string, zodError?: ZodError) }

name: withContract
description: A higher-order function that wraps an AppRouteHandler with Zod-based request validation and typed response helpers. Registers the RouteContract on the handler for later retrieval with getContract. The returned handler validates request body/params/query/headers against the provided Zod schemas and adds res.sendTyped / res.sendValidated methods for type-safe responses.
when_to_use: Defining routes with strict request and response schemas for type safety and documentation
When you need automatic request body/params/query validation with Zod
To generate OpenAPI specs via getContract at build time
avoid: For routes without structured schemas — use plain AppRouteHandler
For error handling contracts — use withErrorContract instead
signature: function withContract<TConfig extends RouteContract>(config: TConfig, settings?: WithContractSettings): (handler: (req: ContractRequest<...>, res: ContractResponse<TConfig>) => unknown) => AppRouteHandler

name: withErrorContract
description: A higher-order function that wraps an AppErrorHandler with a typed error response helper (res.sendError). Registers the ErrorContract on the handler for retrieval with getErrorContract. The wrapped handler receives a res.sendError method that accepts a status code and typed data matching the ErrorContract schema.
when_to_use: Wrapping the global error handler with a typed error response contract
When error responses need to conform to a documented schema
avoid: For route-level contracts — use withContract instead
signature: function withErrorContract<TConfig extends ErrorContract>(config: TConfig): (handler: (err: Error, req: Request, res: ErrorResponse<TConfig>, next: Function) => unknown) => AppErrorHandler

name: ExpressKit Route Definition
description: Describes how to define routes in ExpressKit using the AppRoutes object, including auth policies, middleware hooks, CSP presets, and handler patterns.
when_to_use: When defining HTTP routes for an ExpressKit application
avoid: Using vanilla express.get/post directly — use the routes object for consistent auth and logging
import_statement: import type { AppRoutes, AppRouteDescription, AuthPolicy } from '@gravity-ui/expresskit';
content:
AppRoutes is Record<string, AppRouteDescription | AppRouteHandler>.
Route key format: 'GET /path', 'POST /api/items', 'mount /prefix'

AppRouteDescription fields:
- handler: AppRouteHandler — async (req, res) function
- handlerName?: string — for logging/telemetry
- authPolicy?: AuthPolicy — 'disabled' | 'redirect' | 'unauthorized'
- beforeAuth?: AppMiddleware[] — middleware before auth check
- afterAuth?: AppMiddleware[] — middleware after auth check
- cspPresets?: CSPPreset — route-specific CSP overrides
- enableCaching?: boolean — enable cache headers (default false)
- disableSelfStats?: boolean — skip self-stats telemetry for this route

In route handlers, req.ctx is the per-request AppContext. Use req.ctx.log(), req.ctx.get(REQUEST_ID_PARAM_NAME), etc.
For sub-routers use 'mount /prefix' key with handler: ({router, wrapRouteHandler}) => void.
